target: build-scripts/generate-html.js + test.js — EXP-256 falsifiability module post-integration remediation. Four exact_string_replace patches: (1) reorder Overview to thesis-hero → bar chart → ds-falsifiability-module, replace .falsifiability-module HTML with .ds-falsifiability-module HTML using consistent "0 / N met" framing across all unmet conditions with disambiguating sub-statuses, (2) replace .falsifiability-module CSS with .ds-falsifiability-module CSS bumping status-num to 1.2rem for WCAG AA large-text and switching status-sub to --ink-2 for dark-mode AA, (3) fix factual error in eg-falsifiability ("sub-meter" → "sub-0.01%"), (4) add EXP-271 test block to test.js. ISS-1634 denominator-mismatch handled inside (1) via expanded fm-caption noting "the dome's 69 claimed wins".
scope: Eight issues (ISS-1631..1638) addressed in one EXP per decider grouping. ISS-1634 handled inside the HTML patch via caption text rather than as a separate code change.

patch_segments.anchor_uniqueness: verified unique — the OLD string starts at the bar-chart template literal call (line 1476) and runs through the closing </section> of ds-thesis-hero (line 1535). grep -c on a sentinel inside the OLD string ("class=\"falsifiability-module\" aria-labelledby=\"fm-heading\"") returns 1.
patch_segments.old_string:
${generateVerdictBarChart(tally, wins.length)}

What would change our verdict

Four concrete conditions — any one is sufficient. Full criteria →

  1. Discriminating prediction from dome geometry
    Derive a quantitative result from dome parameters that differs from standard physics for the same observable, then confirm it with measurement.
    0 / ${counts.total}
  2. Genuine prospective prediction
    Specific, quantitative, station-named, timestamped before the data — with the prediction document anchored independently from any observation file.
    1 partialEclipseAug 12, 2026
  3. Derive 7.83 Hz from dome geometry
    Reproduce the Schumann fundamental from dome parameters alone — no globe radius, no spherical-cavity eigenvalue formula.
    0 / ${counts.total}
  4. Sub-5% coordinates without globe inputs
    Reproduce city-to-city distances from dome geometry alone — no WGS84 input, no curve-fitting against globe-measured distances.
    pendingOPEN-001

None of the ${counts.total} predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain.

Live test pending August 12, 2026 eclipse
  • ${counts.total} Claimed wins
  • 0 Survive scrutiny
  • ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}+${silentFailures} Acknowledged + silent failures Details →
  • Wrong side Timestamp error Details →

Four conditions assessed against the dome's ${counts.total} claimed wins — any one is sufficient to upgrade a verdict. Full criteria →

  1. Discriminating prediction from dome geometry
    Derive a quantitative result from dome parameters that differs from standard physics for the same observable, then confirm it with measurement.
    0 / ${counts.total} met2 attempted · 0 succeed
  2. Genuine prospective prediction
    Specific, quantitative, station-named, timestamped before the data — with the prediction document anchored independently from any observation file.
    1 partialeclipse Aug 12, 2026
  3. Derive 7.83 Hz from dome geometry
    Reproduce the Schumann fundamental from dome parameters alone — no globe radius, no spherical-cavity eigenvalue formula.
    0 / ${counts.total} met1 attempted · imports globe radius
  4. Sub-5% coordinates without globe inputs
    Reproduce city-to-city distances from dome geometry alone — no WGS84 input, no curve-fitting against globe-measured distances.

patch_segments.changes_summary: Reorders the Overview triptych to thesis-hero (claim) → bar chart (result) → ds-falsifiability-module (standard). Renames .falsifiability-module → .ds-falsifiability-module and all child .fm-* classes to .ds-fm-* (ISS-1633). Standardises status framing on "0 / ${counts.total} met" for the three unmet conditions (ISS-1632) with disambiguating sub-statuses ("2 attempted · 0 succeed", "1 attempted · imports globe radius", "OPEN-001 · dome-acknowledged") that distinguish "did not attempt" from "attempted and failed" (ISS-1637). Expanded fm-caption explicitly references the 69-WIN denominator so it reads alongside the bar chart's 71-entry caption without ambiguity (ISS-1634). Visual order matches EXP-256 proposal intent (ISS-1638).

patch_segments.changes_summary: Renames every .falsifiability-module / .fm-* class to .ds-falsifiability-module / .ds-fm-* (ISS-1633). Bumps .ds-fm-status-num from 0.95rem to 1.2rem (mobile 1.1rem) so the small-text WCAG AA threshold (4.5:1) becomes the "large text" threshold (3:1) — both the dark-mode --refuted-solid red (#d46a6a, ~4.43:1 vs --card-bg) and --ink-3 gray (~4.23:1) clear 3:1 (ISS-1631). Switches .ds-fm-status-sub color from --ink-3 to --ink-2 (~7.65:1, clears AA at small text) and .ds-fm-status-pending num from --ink-3 to --ink-2. Heading also moved from --ink-3 to --ink-2 for consistency. min-width on status column widened (6rem → 8.5rem) to fit "0 / 69 met". WCAG note: the 1.2rem bump is the in-module fix; site-wide --refuted-solid / --ink-3 dark-mode tokens remain a separate finding for a future cleanup EXP per the curmudgeon's suggestion.

patch_segments.anchor_uniqueness: verified unique — grep -c on the OLD factual string returns 1; the substring "standard geodesy's sub-meter precision" appears nowhere else in the codebase.
patch_segments.old_string: WIN-057's best result (6.2% RMSE) is 600× worse than standard geodesy's sub-meter precision (Misleading)
patch_segments.new_string: WIN-057's best result (6.2% RMSE) is 600× worse than standard geodesy's sub-0.01% precision over similar distances (Misleading)
patch_segments.changes_summary: eg-falsifiability Condition 1 prose says WIN-057's 6.2% RMSE is "600× worse than standard geodesy's sub-meter precision". Math is incoherent: 6.2% on a 10,000 km route = ~620 km error; 620 km / 1 m = 620,000×, not 600×. The 600× ratio is correct only against ~1 km precision, i.e. sub-0.01% — which matches data/wins.json WIN-057 finding ("600× worse than standard geodesy (sub-0.01%)"). Fix aligns the prose with the wins.json source and makes the arithmetic self-consistent.

patch_segments.anchor_uniqueness: anchor is the existing EXP-209 verdict-bar-chart assertions block (5 contiguous lines, line ~683-687). grep -c on the unique sentinel "EXP-209: old .ds-sc-breakdown grid" returns 1. The patch appends EXP-271 assertions immediately after the EXP-209 block; the original EXP-209 lines are preserved verbatim inside the new_string.
patch_segments.changes_summary: Adds 17 new assertions covering: (1) ds-falsifiability-module class present, (2) legacy unprefixed class absent, (3) exactly 4 ds-fm-row, (4) all four condition titles, (5) ds-fm-jump anchor to #eg-falsifiability, (6) consistent "0 / N met" pattern (3 occurrences across conditions 1, 3, 4), (7) sub-status disambiguators present, (8) standalone "pending" removed, (9) status-num font-size 1.2rem (WCAG AA large-text), (10) status-sub color var(--ink-2), (11) Overview visual order thesis → bar → module, (12) eg-falsifiability factual fix present, (13) old "sub-meter precision" wording absent.

issue_mapping.ISS-1631 (WCAG contrast): P2_CSS_BLOCK — bumps status-num to 1.2rem (clears AA large-text 3:1 threshold for --refuted-solid #d46a6a and --ink-3 #8a8680 over --card-bg #222240 in dark mode). Switches status-sub color from --ink-3 to --ink-2 to clear AA at the 0.7rem small-text size. The deeper site-wide token issue (--refuted-solid contrast against various dark-mode backgrounds at small sizes) is left for a future site-wide audit per curmudgeon recommendation; this EXP scopes the fix to the module's new usages.
issue_mapping.ISS-1632 (asymmetric framing): P1_HERO_BLOCK — replaces "pending" gray status on condition 4 with the same "0 / N met" red treatment used by conditions 1 and 3. The semantic distinction between "we evaluated" and "the dome itself acknowledges" is preserved via the disambiguating sub-status text ("OPEN-001 · dome-acknowledged"). Visual symmetry is restored across all three unmet conditions; condition 2 ("1 partial") remains the only differently-coloured row because it genuinely has different status (partial credit, not unmet).
issue_mapping.ISS-1633 (ds- prefix): P1_HERO_BLOCK + P2_CSS_BLOCK — renames every .falsifiability-module / .fm-* class to .ds-falsifiability-module / .ds-fm-* in both HTML and CSS. Aligns with the codebase convention used by .ds-tab-bar, .ds-scorecard, .ds-verdict-bars, .ds-thesis-hero, .ds-evaluate-preface, etc.
issue_mapping.ISS-1634 (denominator mismatch): P1_HERO_BLOCK — expanded ds-fm-caption explicitly references "the dome's 69 claimed wins" so the 69-WIN denominator inside the module is named in the same surface where the reader sees it. The bar chart already has its own caption naming "71 catalog entries = 69 dome-claimed wins plus 2 numbered-collision sub-claims" (generateVerdictBarChart). With both captions explicit, the reader has the bridge: same 69 wins underlying both surfaces, plus 2 sub-claim splits in the bar chart's 71 only.
issue_mapping.ISS-1635 (tests not added): P4_TEST_BLOCK — 17 assertions added to test.js covering presence, count, ordering, framing consistency, WCAG specifics, and the factual fix.
issue_mapping.ISS-1636 (factual error in eg-falsifiabi: P3_FACTUAL_FIX — replaces "standard geodesy's sub-meter precision" with "standard geodesy's sub-0.01% precision over similar distances". Math: 6.2% RMSE on ~10,000 km = ~620 km; 620 km ÷ 1 km = 620× ≈ 600×. Aligns the prose with the data/wins.json WIN-057 finding which already says "(sub-0.01%)".
issue_mapping.ISS-1637 (0 / 69 conflates didn't-attemp: P1_HERO_BLOCK — main status remains "0 / N met" (the empirically true count of dome-claimed wins meeting the bar) but each row now carries a sub-status that names the actual reason: condition 1 "2 attempted · 0 succeed", condition 3 "1 attempted · imports globe radius", condition 4 "OPEN-001 · dome-acknowledged". Reader can scan the column for the headline number and read the sub-status when the distinction matters.
issue_mapping.ISS-1638 (visual order): P1_HERO_BLOCK — moves <section class="ds-thesis-hero"> from after the falsifiability module to before the bar chart. Result order: ds-thesis-hero (claim) → ds-verdict-bars (result distribution) → ds-falsifiability-module (standard for upgrade). Matches EXP-256 proposal's "thesis + bar chart + module" composition intent.

audit_findings.wcag_calculation_detail: Dark mode: --refuted-solid resolves to --semantic-crit = #7a2e2e in light, #d46a6a in dark. --card-bg = #fafafa light / #222240 dark. --ink-2 dark = #b8b5ad. --ink-3 dark = #8a8680. Approximate contrast ratios in dark mode: #d46a6a on #222240 ~4.43:1 (fails AA small-text 4.5:1, clears AA large-text 3:1); #8a8680 on #222240 ~4.23:1 (fails AA small-text, clears AA large-text 3:1); #b8b5ad on #222240 ~7.65:1 (clears AA small-text 4.5:1 and AAA 7:1). Bumping status-num to 1.2rem bold = 19.2px bold ≥ 14pt (18.67px) bold = AA large-text threshold, so the 3:1 ratio applies and both red and gray clear it. Sub-status at 0.72rem stays small-text but now uses --ink-2 which clears 4.5:1.
audit_findings.integration_notes_for_decider: All four patches use exact_string_replace. After applying: run node build.js html then node test.js to confirm 17 new assertions pass. The OLD strings include the `${counts.total}` template-literal placeholders verbatim; do NOT pre-resolve them before applying the patches. Dry-run by analyst against the current generate-html.js + test.js confirmed 18/18 sanity checks pass post-patch.
risks_and_mitigations: Risk: replacing "pending" with red "0 / N met" might be read as "more pessimistic than warranted" for condition 4 since the dome acknowledges the gap (OPEN-001). Mitigation: the sub-status "OPEN-001 · dome-acknowledged" still names the dome's own framing. The red colour reflects "no claim meets the bar today", which is empirically true regardless of who acknowledges what.

Risk: the 1.2rem status-num is visually heavier than the surrounding section captions and may feel oversized. Mitigation: the surrounding context (bar chart count badges at ~1.1rem, ds-tf-number at 1.55rem) supports a 1.2rem number for the key takeaway. Reviewable on a screenshot pass post-build.

Risk: reordering the hero might disturb above-the-fold positioning of the eclipse badge in ds-thesis-hero. Mitigation: thesis-hero is now the FIRST hero block, so the eclipse badge moves UP, which is the intended improvement per HNOTE-007 Item 1.

kernel_of_truth: The dome could legitimately argue that condition 4 is special — the dome itself flags OPEN-001 as work-in-progress, and the curmudgeon defended the asymmetric "pending" treatment for that reason. The argument: "we are not refusing to do the work; we are flagging it open." That is a real semantic distinction. Our response: visual consistency on the unmet column matters more for casual scanning, and the sub-status "OPEN-001 · dome-acknowledged" preserves the open-acknowledgement nuance for any reader who pauses on the row. The red colour communicates the empirically-correct fact ("no demonstration today") and the sub-status carries the "we know, we are working on it" signal. Both pieces of information survive; only the surface treatment is harmonised.
why_kernel_doesnt_save_status_quo: The asymmetric "pending grey" framing was not load-bearing for the open-acknowledgement signal — that signal was carried by the OPEN-001 sub-status, which we preserve. Stripping the gray-out treatment loses nothing the sub-status doesn't already carry, while gaining visual consistency that helps the casual reader who scans the column for "what does the unmet bar look like".

anticipated_objections.objection: You moved the thesis-hero away from where it was integrated. Is that authorised?
anticipated_objections.response: Per ISS-1638, the original EXP-256 proposal's composition was thesis-hero (claim) → bar chart (result) → falsifiability-module (standard). The current order ended up inverted (bar chart → module → thesis-hero) because EXP-255 was integrated after EXP-256's anchors had already been resolved. The fix restores the proposal's explicit intent.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why bump status-num to 1.2rem instead of using a more saturated red token?
anticipated_objections.response: A more saturated --refuted-solid in dark mode would clear small-text AA but ripple across the entire site (verdict bars, definition cards, etc.). The 1.2rem bump is scoped to the module and crosses the AA "large text" threshold (14pt bold = 18.67px), so the contrast requirement drops from 4.5:1 to 3:1 and both the red and the gray clear it. A site-wide --refuted-solid review remains a sensible follow-up but is out of scope for this EXP per the curmudgeon's recommendation.

anticipated_objections.objection: Adding "across 69 claimed wins" to the module caption is verbose.
anticipated_objections.response: It's 6 words. Removing them re-opens ISS-1634 (denominator mismatch). The bar chart's caption already names both denominators; the module's caption doing the same gives the reader a complete bridge.

anticipated_objections.objection: The eg-falsifiability factual fix changes the rhetorical punch ("sub-meter" sounds tighter than "sub-0.01%").
anticipated_objections.response: Yes — and "sub-meter" is also wrong by a factor of ~1000. Rhetorical punch is not worth being mathematically incoherent. "Sub-0.01% over similar distances" gives the reader the actual industry-standard precision context.

acceptance_criteria: docs/index.html contains exactly 4 elements with class="ds-fm-row"
No occurrence of class="falsifiability-module" (unprefixed) remains in docs/index.html
All four condition titles are present
Status-num strings: "0 / 69 met" appears 3× (conditions 1, 3, 4); "1 partial" appears 1× (condition 2)
Sub-status disambiguators present: "2 attempted", "1 attempted · imports globe radius", "OPEN-001 · dome-acknowledged"
Overview visual order: ds-thesis-hero appears BEFORE ds-verdict-bars, which appears BEFORE ds-falsifiability-module
eg-falsifiability prose contains "sub-0.01% precision over similar distances" (factual fix)
eg-falsifiability prose does NOT contain "sub-meter precision"
node test.js shows the 17 new EXP-271 assertions passing
Light and dark mode both render correctly (no hardcoded hex)
Mobile viewport 600px collapses to 2-column with 1.1rem status-num

decider_instructions.integration_step_1: Apply P1_HERO_BLOCK via exact_string_replace in build-scripts/generate-html.js. Verify with: grep -c 'class="ds-falsifiability-module"' build-scripts/generate-html.js (expect 1) AND grep -n 'class="ds-thesis-hero"' build-scripts/generate-html.js should show a line BEFORE the generateVerdictBarChart call.
decider_instructions.integration_step_3: Apply P3_FACTUAL_FIX. Verify: grep -c "sub-0.01% precision over similar distances" build-scripts/generate-html.js (expect 1) AND grep -c "sub-meter precision" build-scripts/generate-html.js (expect 0).
decider_instructions.integration_step_4: Apply P4_TEST_BLOCK in test.js. Verify: grep -c "EXP-271:" test.js (expect ≥17).
decider_instructions.integration_step_5: Run node build.js html. Verify docs/index.html now contains class="ds-falsifiability-module" exactly once.
decider_instructions.integration_step_6: Run node test.js — expect previous tests + 17 new EXP-271 assertions, all passing.
decider_instructions.integration_step_7: Run tests/run.sh — full suite. Expect green.
decider_instructions.rollback_plan: If any patch fails to apply (anchor mismatch from concurrent edits), revert all four patches. Do not leave a partial state — the test block will fail without the HTML/CSS changes and vice versa.

cross_references.related_directive: DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 (truth-vs-standards rubric — "ISS-1633 ds- prefix" is style-guide territory and was relaxed by curmudgeon; we still address it because the decider grouped it into this EXP)

newsworthy: Falsifiability module fixes: every unmet condition now reads the same way, the WCAG contrast issue is closed, and the math behind WIN-057's "600× worse" claim now actually adds up.
notes_for_decider: Eight-issue cluster collapses into four exact_string_replace patches. All anchors verified unique. The eg-falsifiability factual fix is independent of the other patches and can land separately if the others have to be reverted. ISS-1634 is closed as part of P1_HERO_BLOCK via the expanded fm-caption — no separate code change needed because the bar chart caption already names both denominators.
tldr_evidence: Curmudgeon review of EXP-256 plus post-integration audit found eight issues: WCAG contrast, asymmetric "pending" vs "0/69" framing, missing ds- prefix on classes, denominator mismatch, missing tests, factual error in linked eg-falsifiability prose, "0/69" conflating did-not-attempt with attempted-and-failed, and visual order inverted from proposal intent.
tldr_verdict: Four exact_string_replace patches: reorder Overview to thesis → bar chart → falsifiability module, rename .fm-* to .ds-fm-* with consistent "0 / N met" framing and disambiguating sub-statuses, fix eg-falsifiability "sub-meter" → "sub-0.01%", and add 17 regression assertions.
